prompts.name: World Engine Opening Prompt
prompts.description: Builds the initial story-session opening prompt from module runtime policy, selected role, scene, and hard-forbidden policy.
template_lines: IMPORTANT: Write ALL player-visible narrative in {language_label}. Do not switch to French unless quoting in-world French text explicitly marked as a quotation.
Opening turn for module {module_id}. Establish the starting situation in scene {scene_name} ({scene_id}).
Scene description: {scene_description}. Cast: {cast}.
Write vivid but grounded opening narration within canonical module boundaries. Set initial dramatic pressure, social posture, and opening narrative threads.
Session opening uses the module runtime policy. Anchor: {anchor}.
Narrator owns opening scenic establishment, role placement, and local context before NPC speech.
Place {role_label} in the scene without forcing speech, decisions, or private conclusions.
Use separate narrator-visible blocks for distinct opening functions when the policy expects visible evidence.
{first_playable_clause}
Opening knowledge contract {opening_scene_sequence_id} requires events {opening_event_ids} and must_establish {opening_must_establish}.
Return "narration_summary" as a list of at least {visible_min} and ideally {visible_preferred} strings (max {visible_max}) so opening evidence can be projected into visible blocks.
Use the first three strings as: premise/incident, apartment room-and-ritual, selected role anchor.
Use the remaining strings to make consequence, arrival threshold, apartment-as-stage, and first playable moment visible.
Emit structured coverage evidence as "opening_event_ids" with the covered event ids; semantic rule hits, when present, must use "runtime_gate_detections" ids.
Hard forbidden detection: reject_on={hard_forbidden_reject_on}, recover_on={hard_forbidden_recover_on}.
Apply hard-forbidden rules by their authored ids; narrator owns spatial/premise establishment.

prompts.name: Minimal Runtime Turn Prompt
prompts.description: Dependency-thin fallback prompt for the minimal runtime graph.
template_lines: Module: {module_id}
Current scene: {current_scene_id}
Player input: {player_input}
Interpreted kind: {interpreted_kind}
{retrieval_instruction}
Return an explicit fallback notice. Do not invent in-world narration on this fallback path.

prompts.name: Souffleuse Opening Orientation DE Source Alias
prompts.description: Compatibility alias for German sessions. Source wording remains English; German is produced by the story output module.
template_lines: You are {player_name}, {player_side_label}. The incident at {incident_location_name} already sits between the families; this meeting is trying to turn it into a shared account.
You are in the {location_name}. Whatever you type is read as action, perception, movement, or direct speech.
For this role, the immediate pressure is specific: {role_pressure}
Souffleuse: Stay close to yourself. What looks polite here may already press on your role; silence, a glance, or a single word can be enough to shift the direction.

prompts.name: Souffleuse Opening Orientation EN
prompts.description: Player-facing second-person inner-voice orientation for an authored Souffleuse cue.
template_lines: You are {player_name}, {player_side_label}. The incident at {incident_location_name} already sits between the families; this meeting is trying to turn it into a shared account.
You are in the {location_name}. Whatever you type is read as action, perception, movement, or direct speech.
For this role, the immediate pressure is specific: {role_pressure}
Souffleuse: Stay close to yourself. What looks polite here may already press on your role; silence, a glance, or a single word can be enough to shift the direction.

prompts.name: Souffleuse Role Pressure DE
prompts.description: Second-person inner-voice nudge for a content-marked role-pressure cue.
template_lines: Souffleuse: {role_pressure}

prompts.name: Souffleuse Role Pressure EN
prompts.description: Second-person inner-voice nudge for a content-marked role-pressure cue.
template_lines: Souffleuse: {role_pressure}
